rationale: The citing paper explicitly uses Naylor et al. (2011) to support a key premise in its theory development: review readers infer reviewer characteristics when source identity is unknown or ambiguous. That premise directly motivates the paper's own inference-based mechanism, where arousal cues shape perceived reviewer effort and thus helpfulness. The citation is also revisited in the discussion on identity disclosure and assumed similarity, showing conceptual use beyond a passing background list.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Naylor et al. (2011) as conceptual groundwork for how consumers infer properties of anonymous reviewers. It builds on that inference logic to develop a different downstream mechanism, linking expressed arousal to effort inferences and perceived review helpfulness.
